channel.title: FrontionNews - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Major 7.5-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Off Japan, Tsunami Warning Issued
item.description: A powerful 7.5-magnitude earthquake struck off the northeastern coast of Japan, prompting a tsunami warning with waves of up to 3 meters expected. Bullet train operations were halted and residents were urged to evacuate coastal areas.

item.title: Italy Discusses Expanding EU Naval Mission to Protect Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Italy's defense minister said discussions are underway to expand the EU's Aspides naval mission to include the Strait of Hormuz, warning that Iran's missile and nuclear ambitions pose a global threat. The minister stressed that Asian countries should assume greater responsibility given the strait's vital importance to their economies.

item.title: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Former President Wins Election by Landslide
item.description: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's election by a landslide, official results showed, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member state closer to Moscow. Radev may secure a parliamentary majority, ending years of weak coalition governments.

item.title: China Warns Joint US-Philippines-Japan Drills Risk Regional Trust
item.description: China warned that joint military drills involving the US, Philippines and Japan risk undermining regional trust, as more than 17,000 troops launched the Balikatan exercises. Beijing said the Asia-Pacific region needs peace and tranquility, not the introduction of external forces.

item.title: Iran Studied Ukraine War to Sharpen Drone and AI Capabilities Before US Operation
item.description: Iranian military commanders closely studied the war in Ukraine before launching operations against the US, focusing on drones, 3D printing and AI-powered systems. The FT reports Tehran applied lessons from Kyiv's asymmetric warfare to prepare for conflict with Washington.

item.title: Iran to Send Negotiation Team to Islamabad Tuesday Despite Blockade Tensions
item.description: An Iranian delegation will arrive in Islamabad on Tuesday for a second round of peace talks, Pakistani official sources told Nikkei Asia, despite Tehran's earlier statements that it would not send negotiators while the US navy maintains its blockade. The mediator says the focus is on ceasefire extension as 'complex issues' require more time.

item.title: Asian Financial Regulators Sound Alert Over Anthropic's Mythos AI Security Risks
item.description: Regulators across Asia are stepping up scrutiny of cybersecurity risks in financial systems as concerns spread over Anthropic's latest AI model Mythos. Singapore's financial regulator is urging banks to plug holes, while South Korea convened an emergency meeting after Anthropic found the model capable of discovering security holes that had gone undetected for years.

item.title: Germany Begins Privatization of Seized Gazprom Unit SEFE
item.description: The German government will begin the official privatization of SEFE, the state entity formed from Gazprom's seized German unit. Berlin plans to raise 1.5-2 billion euros to expand the company's energy infrastructure, with SEFE having already secured long-term LNG supply deals with Norway's Equinor and UAE's ADNOC to reduce dependence on Russian gas.

item.title: Asia's EV Revolution Accelerates as Iran War Oil Shock Hits Pump Prices
item.description: With Brent crude hovering around $100 per barrel amid the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruption, Asian nations heavily dependent on oil imports are accelerating their shift to electric vehicles. Nepal now ranks second globally for EV sales share at over 70% of new vehicle sales, as the combination of abundant hydropower and high fossil fuel taxes makes petrol cars a luxury few see the point in buying.

item.title: Iran Agrees to Attend Second Round of US Talks Despite Hormuz Hostilities
item.description: An Iranian delegation is expected to arrive in Islamabad on Tuesday for the second round of talks with the US, according to Pakistani sources, despite ongoing Strait of Hormuz hostilities. The talks come as the fragile ceasefire faces strain from the US seizure of an Iranian cargo vessel and mutual accusations of violations.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Attack on Russia's Tuapse Port Kills One
item.description: A major Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse sparked a fire and killed at least one person, Russian officials said, just hours after a blaze was doused in the wake of a similar attack on April 16. The strike is part of Kyiv's campaign targeting Russian energy export infrastructure.

item.title: National Australia Bank Flags $503 Million Impairment Hit From Mideast Volatility
item.description: Australia's largest business lender said it expects credit impairment charges to double to A$706 million ($503 million) in the first half, as the Iran war roils the global economy and financial markets. The announcement reflects growing concerns about the conflict's impact on banking sector stability.

item.title: Bank Indonesia to Hold Rates at 4.75% Through 2026 as Iran War Fuels Inflation Risks
item.description: Bank Indonesia is set to keep its key policy rate unchanged at 4.75%, according to a Reuters poll of 31 economists, with many paring bets for cuts this year as an Iran war-fuelled energy shock pushes up inflation and pressures the rupiah. Emerging markets face tightening monetary conditions amid the crisis.

item.title: European Shares Set to Open Lower as Hopes for US-Iran Peace Fade
item.description: Futures tracking Europe's main stock indexes fell more than 1% as hopes for peace in the Middle East ebbed with tensions reigniting after Washington seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate. European markets face uncertainty as the ceasefire appears increasingly fragile.

item.title: UAE Bourses Ease on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
item.description: UAE markets retreated in early trade as fears deepened that the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran could unravel after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel, while traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely suspended. Gulf equities remain volatile amid the ongoing crisis.

item.title: Pope Leo Condemns 'Tyrants' Spending Billions on Wars During Africa Visit
item.description: Pope Leo XIV criticized 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars during his visit to conflict-hit Cameroon, saying 'the world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants.' The pontiff also criticized foreigners who exploit Africa's wealth for profit, days after a public spat with President Trump over the Iran war.

item.title: Iran Executes Two Men Convicted of Links to Israel's Mossad Spy Agency
item.description: Iran executed two men convicted of cooperating with Israel's Mossad intelligence service and planning attacks inside the country. The two, identified as Mohammad Masoum Shahi and Hamed Validi, were accused of belonging to a spy network and had received training abroad including in Iraq's Kurdistan region.

item.title: Four Candidates Vie to Lead Embattled UN Amid Global Crises and Budget Woes
item.description: Four candidates are competing to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN Secretary-General, facing unprecedented global instability, wars and a crushing budget crisis. Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan, and Senegal's Macky Sall will face grillings by 193 member states.

item.title: US and Philippines Kick Off Major Combat Drills Despite Iran War Focus
item.description: The United States and the Philippines kicked off one of their largest combat exercises Monday, demonstrating continued US military commitment to the Indo-Pacific region despite Washington's focus on the Iran war. The annual Balikatan exercises come amid rising tensions with China in the South China Sea.

item.title: Pope Leo Criticizes Foreigners Exploiting Africa's Wealth During Cameroon Visit
item.description: Pope Leo XIV criticized foreigners who 'in the name of profit, continue to lay their hands on the African continent to exploit and plunder it' during his visit to Bamenda, Cameroon. The pontiff addressed 20,000 worshippers in a city at the center of a brutal separatist rebellion that has killed at least 6,000 people.

item.title: North Korea Tests Ballistic Missiles With Cluster Munition Warheads
item.description: North Korea test-launched five Hwasong-11 Ra ballistic missiles armed with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads, with Kim Jong Un and his daughter observing. The tests highlight Pyongyang's push to develop weapons capable of penetrating US and South Korean defenses, potentially inspired by cluster munitions use in the Iran war.

item.title: Iran Rejects Second Round of US Peace Talks Citing 'Excessive Demands'
item.description: Iran's state news agency IRNA denied participation in new peace talks with the US, citing Washington's 'excessive demands, unrealistic expectations' and the continued naval blockade. The diplomatic setback came as JD Vance and Jared Kushner were reportedly preparing to travel to Islamabad for negotiations.

item.title: Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Standoff Blocks Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 6% in early trading after Iran reversed its decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. US crude rose to $87.90 per barrel and Brent climbed to $95.64, wiping out much of Friday's 9% decline as renewed doubts emerged about when the vital waterway will fully reopen.

item.title: Trump Says US Seized Iranian Cargo Ship After Blockade Breach Attempt
item.description: President Trump said US forces seized the Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska after it ignored warnings and attempted to breach a US naval blockade. Iran's military called the seizure 'armed piracy' and vowed retaliation, as the fragile ceasefire set to expire Wednesday faces growing strain from mutual accusations of violations.

item.title: Ukraine Deploys Robots to Replace Human Soldiers in Combat Assault Missions
item.description: Ukraine's military is increasingly using robots to replace human soldiers in combat, including assault missions. The unmanned ground vehicles and drones allow Kyiv to preserve manpower while maintaining pressure on Russian forces, marking a significant evolution in modern warfare tactics.
